scope_delivery.explanation: No — AUDIT-HT not delivered. S-0117 routine fire halted post-eager-claim mid-authoring of hubs_and_traces.md (likely context cap on the largest of 12 evidence deliverables: 96 edges + 10 nodes + 3 syllabus traces). No deliverable produced; no close commit; routine wedged with current_status: in_progress and AUDIT-HT status: in_progress. Administratively closed with status: closed_void per Issue #58 wedge-resolution (no slot consumed beyond the eager-claim ledger entry). AUDIT-HT decomposed into AUDIT-HT-1 (hubs 1–5) and AUDIT-HT-2 (hubs 6–10 + 3 traces) in the same commit so the next routine fire picks a smaller deliverable under context cap. Systemic wedge-detect-and-pause mechanism (per ADR 0060) lands in the same close session so no future halted-routine wedge spams hourly HANDOFF entries.

outcome_summary: S-0117 routine fire for AUDIT-HT eager-claimed at 2026-05-10T03:00Z (commit e0b4a27) and halted post-eager-claim mid-authoring of hubs_and_traces.md, leaving register_state.json current_status: in_progress and auto_target.json[AUDIT-HT].status: in_progress with no deliverable and no archive. Per Issue #58 the wedge spawned hourly HANDOFF spam from subsequent routine fires that found no eligible task. Administratively closed by interactive recovery: archive populated with closed_void status (no slot consumed; this entry exists for ledger continuity); register_state.json current_status flipped closed; auto_target.json AUDIT-HT decomposed into AUDIT-HT-1 (hubs 1–5, ~48 edges) + AUDIT-HT-2 (hubs 6–10 + 3 traces, depends on AUDIT-HT-1) so the next routine fire claims a smaller deliverable under context cap. The same close session lands the systemic fix (ADR 0060 wedge-detect-and-pause + routine_wedge_detect.py tool + routine-mode-lifecycle skill update) so future halted-routine wedges auto-detect and exit cleanly rather than spam HANDOFF.
